title: Name age caste vocabulary and rebirth casting remain unresolved
source_observation: The poem provides Aputhiran's origin education exclusion service and rebirth but modern equivalents remain uncertain
risk: The adaptation could invent a fixed caste biography exact age or permanent-soul visual identity
decision: Keep terms provisional cast life stages safely and decide same-actor rebirth only after Tamil Buddhist and performance review
rationale: Protects textual honesty and present-life distinction
required_review: Classical Tamil; caste history; Buddhist studies; casting; publicity

title: Compassion begins before the miraculous vessel
source_observation: Aputhiran begs distributes ordinary food feeds vulnerable people eats leftovers and sleeps beside his pot before receiving Amudhasurabhi
risk: The miracle could erase labour and turn ethics into divine selection
decision: Give substantial screen time to collection serving cleanup and recipient relationships before the vessel appears
rationale: Establishes that the object amplifies an existing practice rather than creating virtue
required_review: Screenplay; food systems; disability access; performance; editor

title: Sali's sexual history cannot determine Aputhiran's moral worth
source_observation: Sali's history and abandonment are used publicly to disqualify her son
risk: The film could repeat misogynistic shame or make her a corrupt origin for a pure hero
decision: Show danger travel limited options and weaponised ancestry while keeping her actions open to accountability and compassion
rationale: Prevents maternal sexual stigma from becoming narrative truth
required_review: Classical Tamil; gender history; trauma review; screenplay

title: Aputhiran is learned and critiques exclusion from within knowledge
source_observation: His adoptive household educates him and he answers hostile claims with learned examples
risk: He could be simplified into an innocent anti-intellectual outsider speaking modern slogans
decision: Preserve memory argument humour and knowledge while distinguishing literary critique from modern ideology
rationale: Makes his dissent specific to character and text
required_review: Classical Tamil; history of religions; dialogue; actor preparation

title: The sacrificial-cow sequence protects animals without flattening all ritual traditions
source_observation: The poem presents a frightened decorated cow Aputhiran's intervention and a hostile dispute
risk: The scene could use real distress graphic violence or claim documentary truth about all Vedic ritual
decision: Use strict animal-welfare methods preserve the poem's polemic and label exact ritual reconstruction as unresolved
rationale: Protects animals historical complexity and religious dignity
required_review: Animal welfare; Vedic ritual history; classical Tamil; stunt; legal

title: Recipients remain agents rather than devotional evidence
source_observation: Aputhiran calls socially vulnerable people to eat and later feeds people animals and birds
risk: Crowds could exist only to praise the saint or display suffering
decision: Create recurring recipients with preferences correction humour access needs and reciprocal concern without inventing them as textual persons
rationale: Preserves dignity and makes feeding dramatically relational
required_review: Disability consultants; poverty ethics; screenplay; casting; extras coordination

title: The Amudhasurabhi does not erase distribution labour and infrastructure
source_observation: The vessel produces abundant food but feeding still occurs in a material social space
risk: A glowing prop could imply that water serving order sanitation transport and labour solve themselves
decision: Show logistics access cleaning vessels shelter and bodily work in every large feeding sequence
rationale: Connects miracle to the project's hunger and infrastructure system
required_review: Food safety; production design; accessibility; continuity; VFX

title: Prosperity is good even when it destabilises Aputhiran's identity
source_observation: Indra's rain removes famine and leaves Aputhiran without recipients
risk: The film could imply hunger should continue so compassionate people retain purpose or portray former recipients as ungrateful
decision: Celebrate improved food security while showing Aputhiran's personal disorientation as a separate problem
rationale: Protects the anti-hunger ethic and reveals his service dependency
required_review: Screenplay; ethics; psychology consultant; editor; music

title: Self-starvation remains ethically tragic as well as sacred within the poem
source_observation: Aputhiran relinquishes the vessel and then fasts to death on Manipallavam within a karmic narrative
risk: The death could become beautiful instruction moral reward or proof that self-erasure is compassion
decision: Preserve sacred context and agency while showing alternatives physical cost isolation and unresolved tragedy without procedural detail
rationale: Honours the text without romanticising self-harm
required_review: Buddhist studies; safeguarding; mental-health consultant; direction; edit; publicity

title: Punniyarajan is karmic continuity not an identical present self
source_observation: The poem connects Aputhiran's death cow-affection and merit with rebirth as Punniyarajan
risk: Same-face or direct-memory shorthand could erase the king's distinct present life or imply an unchanged permanent soul
decision: Treat bodily echoes casting and memory as production choices and preserve different upbringing knowledge and obligations
rationale: Supports Buddhist causation while protecting character individuality
required_review: Buddhist studies; classical Tamil; casting; VFX; Punniyarajan dossier
